For the benefit of those who do not have the book of Mormon, and to stir up those who have, to peruse its valua- ble pages more strictly, we extract the following chapter.
And ndw I speak…

in the days of my greatest sorrow Joseph is the last born in the wilderness. The church had gone into the wilderness (see Revelation 12). Joseph is born during the greatest sorrow, the great apostasy. There is a promise that the seed will not be destroyed.

I will read a portion of a prophecy, written in the Book of Mormon, in the third chapter of the second book of Nephi. The Prophet who spoke the words I am about to read, and who also quoted the words of another Prophet, was named Lehi; he lived about six hundred years before Christ. “For behold, thou art the fruit of my loins; and I am a descendant of Joseph who was carried captive into Egypt.…

The ancient apocrypha like the Book of Mormon give a peculiar importance to the figure of Joseph, who is both a real person and a symbol. Since Joseph is "a type and a shadow," we find a whole line of Josephs. "I have led this people forth out of . . . Jerusalem," says the Lord through the prophet Jacob " . . . that I might raise up unto me a righteous branch from the fruit of the loins of…

exodus of the pioneers. An obvious likeness is that both groups had their inland sea of salt water and a Jordan River. But there were many other very significant similarities. Ancient Israel and modern Israel are linked arm in arm. The Josephs Ancient Israel had leaders before Moses, and modern Israel had a prophet-president before Brigham Young. Proving to the world that the holy scriptures are true. Here we are told that one of the primary purposes of the Book of Mormon is to prove that the Bible is true. This same message was given to Joseph of Egypt when the Lord told him of the mission and ministry of the Prophet Joseph Smith. "Unto him will I give power to bring forth my word unto the seed of thy loins," the ancient Joseph was told,…

"I will raise up unto the fruit of thy loins" corrected text "I will raise up one unto the fruit of thy loins" (Found in the original manuscript, Royal Skousen , Critical text project of the Book of Mormon)

I will read a portion of a prophecy, written in the Book of Mormon, in the third chapter of the second book of Nephi. The Prophet who spoke the words I am about to read, and who also quoted the words of another Prophet, was named Lehi; he lived about six hundred years before Christ. “For behold, thou art the fruit of my loins; and I am a descendant of Joseph who was carried captive into Egypt.…

Proving to the world that the holy scriptures are true. Here we are told that one of the primary purposes of the Book of Mormon is to prove that the Bible is true. This same message was given to Joseph of Egypt when the Lord told him of the mission and ministry of the Prophet Joseph Smith. "Unto him will I give power to bring forth my word unto the seed of thy loins," the ancient Joseph was told,…

The ancient apocrypha like the Book of Mormon give a peculiar importance to the figure of Joseph, who is both a real person and a symbol. Since Joseph is "a type and a shadow," we find a whole line of Josephs. "I have led this people forth out of . . . Jerusalem," says the Lord through the prophet Jacob " . . . that I might raise up unto me a righteous branch from the fruit of the loins of…
